title: The Carlton's Memory

activity.name: Carlton Hotel Facade Investigation — La Croisette

narrative_intro:
Maro's voice is still in your head — the tremor when she said her father's name, the steel underneath it. She sent you a fragment of Kask's ledger: a column of Ethereum wallet addresses, each one tagged with a date and a property sale in Cannes. The first entry is dated July 28, 2013 — the same day armed men walked into the Carlton InterContinental and walked out with €103 million in jewels. That's not a coincidence. That's a genesis block.

The Carlton sits on La Croisette like a gilded jaw. You've seen it in photographs — the Belle Époque cupolas, the white facade, the kind of architecture that makes theft feel theatrical. But Kask didn't care about theatre. He cared about what happened AFTER. According to his partial ledger, within 72 hours of the heist, three shell companies were registered in Cyprus. Within six months, those companies purchased apartments visible from where you're about to stand. The jewels became crypto became concrete. And right now, Enzo Castaldi — the man who knows every stone on this boulevard — is offering to walk you through exactly which buildings. He's been texting you since the port. Charming. Knowledgeable. Eager.

Specter just sent a single line: 'Count the cupolas. Not the ones you see.'

You have no idea what that means. Yet.
instruction: Walk east along La Croisette from the Vieux Port toward the Carlton Hotel (3-4 minutes). When you arrive at the Carlton facade, photograph the building's distinctive twin cupolas from across the boulevard. Then: both you AND another player in the quest must independently count the number of balcony levels visible on the central facade between the two cupolas and report the same number to Maro via the app. Kask embedded a two-validator consensus check — if two independent observers confirm the same architectural detail, it unlocks the next ledger fragment. This is blockchain logic applied to the real world. Maro is waiting.
player_action: Photograph the Carlton Hotel's twin cupolas and central facade from across Boulevard de la Croisette. Count the exact number of distinct balcony levels visible between the two cupolas on the main facade. Report this number through the app. Your count must match another player's independent observation to unlock the next ledger fragment — Kask designed a real-world consensus mechanism.

gps_trigger.content_brief: A partial page from Kask's forensic ledger auto-unlocks in the vault. It shows three Ethereum wallet addresses tagged 'CARLTON-GENESIS' with timestamps from July 2013, each linked to a Cyprus shell company name and a Cannes property address on or near La Croisette. One address is redacted — the validator fragment needed to reveal it is locked behind the consensus check. A handwritten annotation in Kask's writing reads: 'The cupolas are a lie. One was rebuilt in 2009. Ask yourself who paid for the renovation and why their name appears on line 7.'

camera_prompt: The player photographs the Carlton InterContinental Hotel's iconic facade, focusing on the twin Belle Époque cupolas and the balconied central section. AI vision identifies the cupolas, balcony levels, architectural details, and any renovation differences between the two domes. The AI character (Castaldi) uses real architectural features to weave in narrative about which apartments visible from this vantage point were purchased with laundered crypto-converted jewel proceeds, making the real Croisette skyline feel like a crime scene map.

tension.description: Castaldi floods the player with useful-seeming architectural intel about the Carlton and surrounding buildings, naming real streets and dates, building trust rapidly. Meanwhile Specter's cryptic cupola message and the vault document hint that Castaldi's knowledge is suspiciously precise — how does a 'local historian' know the exact renovation timeline of a hotel cupola linked to money laundering? The collaborative consensus requirement forces the player to commit to an observation, creating the first irreversible data point in the ledger reconstruction.
tension.resolution: Whether the player's count matches or not, Maro unlocks a partial ledger fragment. If it matches, the fragment is complete and reveals the redacted address — which turns out to be a building Castaldi personally brokered the sale of in 2017. If it fails, the fragment is partial but Maro manually overrides with a comment: 'Close enough. My father taught me that consensus is a negotiation, not a religion.'

verification.target: Number of distinct balcony levels on the Carlton's central facade between the twin cupolas
verification.success_condition: Player's count matches the second validator's independent count (the real answer is 7 distinct balcony levels on the central facade)
verification.success_reaction: Full ledger fragment 3 unlocks in the vault, revealing the redacted Croisette property address — which is a building Castaldi brokered. Maro's trust increases significantly. Specter sends a rare second message: 'You see clearly. That matters more than you know.' +15 XP bonus.
verification.failure_fallback: Maro manually overrides the consensus with Kask's backup key, unlocking a partial fragment missing one property address. She texts: 'Consensus is a negotiation. We keep moving. But you're flying with one eye closed now — that missing address will matter at the end.' Story continues with slightly less information available for the final validation decision.
verification.timeout_reaction: After 6 minutes, Castaldi texts: 'Still admiring the view? I don't blame you. But time is a factor today, remember? Send Maro your count — any count. Kask's system accepts approximations if you're honest about your confidence level. That's the difference between blockchain and bureaucracy.'

blockchain_event: FRAGMENT_3_CONSENSUS — Ledger fragment 3 (Volkov-Croisette property chain) unlocked via meatspace consensus protocol. Two independent validators confirmed matching architectural observation. Smart contract state: 3/6 fragments recovered. Castre Protocol auto-destruct timer: 2h47m remaining.
unlock_message: CONSENSUS PROTOCOL ACTIVE — Kask's ledger requires two independent validators to confirm the same real-world observation. Your count has been submitted. Waiting for second confirmation. Fragment 3 of the Castre Protocol is assembling.
